weekly_template.name: AI Recommended — Bryson Pope — Power — 2026-07-01
weekly_template.description: AI-generated weekly program. Focus: Power. Source: ai_layer.recommended_programs id=123.

activity_templates.name: Prep — Bryson Pope
activity_templates.description: Bryson's prep block is designed to improve pitching mechanics by targeting his deficits in total trunk flexion and shoulder abduction. The selection prioritizes thoracic spine and hip mobility, core and scapular activation, and includes power-focused movement enhancement drills to prime him for his session.

exercises.exercise_name: Knee Hug to Lateral Lunge w/ Arms OH
exercises.matched_exercise_name: Knee Hug to Lateral Lunge w/ Arms OH
exercises.notes: This dynamic movement elevates body temperature and improves hip mobility, preparing for the session's demands.

exercises.exercise_name: Pull Back Butt Kick to Walking Spiderman w/ Hip Lift and Reach
exercises.matched_exercise_name: Pull Back Butt Kick to Walking Spiderman w/ Hip Lift and Reach
exercises.notes: A comprehensive dynamic warm-up that opens up hips, hamstrings, and thoracic spine for improved movement quality.
Addresses total trunk flexion deficits by improving hip internal rotation and thoracic spine rotation, crucial for pitching mechanics.

exercises.exercise_name: All Fours Band T Spine Rotation
exercises.matched_exercise_name: All Fours Band T Spine Rotation
exercises.notes: Specifically targets thoracic spine rotation, directly improving the total trunk flexion deficit and enhancing rotational power.
Enhances hip internal and external rotation, which is foundational for efficient lower body mechanics and hip-shoulder separation in pitching.
Improves hip external rotation and general hip mobility, supporting better pelvic control during the pitching delivery.

exercises.exercise_name: Single Leg Hip Thrust Off Wall
exercises.matched_exercise_name: Single Leg Hip Thrust Off Wall
exercises.notes: Activates the glutes and improves single-leg stability, essential for generating power and controlling the lower half in pitching.
Enhances scapular stability and control in overhead movements, crucial for addressing shoulder abduction deficits and overall arm health.
Engages deep core muscles to improve trunk stability, supporting efficient transfer of force during rotational movements.
Prepares the posterior chain for dynamic movement, improving hamstring flexibility and body awareness for integrated movement.
Focuses on diaphragmatic breathing to enhance core stability and nervous system regulation, readying the body for high-intensity work.
Primes the body for rotational power by engaging the core and hips, directly supporting a power-focused training session.

exercises.exercise_name: SS Anti Rotation MB Scoop Toss
exercises.matched_exercise_name: SS Anti Rotation MB Scoop Toss
exercises.notes: Develops anti-rotational core strength and explosive hip-to-shoulder separation, crucial for effective pitching mechanics.
A pitcher-specific movement pattern drill that primes the throwing motion and enhances neural pathways for improved on-field performance.

activity_templates.name: Bulletproofing — Bryson Pope
activity_templates.description: Bryson exhibits deficits in total trunk flexion and limited shoulder abduction/horizontal abduction, which are critical for efficient pitching mechanics. The selected exercises prioritize pitcher staples for general arm health while also targeting improved thoracic and hip mobility to address trunk flexion, and enhanced scapular control and shoulder ROM to improve abduction patterns.

exercises.notes: A pitcher staple that improves hip mobility, supporting better trunk mechanics and indirectly addressing trunk flexion deficits.
Enhances thoracic spine rotation and hip mobility, crucial for addressing the total trunk flexion deficit and improving throwing efficiency.
Improves thoracic mobility and shoulder horizontal abduction range, directly addressing the deficits in peak abduction and max horizontal abduction.
A critical pitcher staple for improving posterior shoulder capsule mobility and internal rotation, essential for arm health and preventing impingement.
Activates mid and lower traps to enhance scapular stability and control, directly supporting improved shoulder abduction mechanics.
Addresses pectoral tightness and improves external rotation, which can be restricted and contribute to poor shoulder mechanics during abduction.
Promotes core and scapular stability through serratus anterior activation, supporting overall trunk control and scapular function for throwing.
A pitcher staple for shoulder decompression and improving overhead mobility, contributing to overall shoulder health and resilience.

activity_templates.name: Plyo — Bryson Pope
activity_templates.description: This three-day plyo cycle for Bryson Pope is designed to address his primary deficits in total trunk flexion, peak abduction, and maximum horizontal abduction, with a focus on improving hip drive, lead leg mechanics, and a more efficient arm path. As he is in the Velocity Phase, all plyo levels (P0, P1, P2) are permitted without phase-based downgrades. The P0 session prioritizes recovery and gentle pattern reinforcement. P1 introduces balanced feel with moderate intent, while P2 is geared towards maximal intent expression and aggressive correction of his identified biomechanical gaps to translate to higher velocity on the mound.

sessions.label: Recovery day
sessions.session_intent: Focus on active recovery, promoting tissue restoration, and re-establishing foundational movement patterns with low intent. Emphasize connection and feel without pushing intensity.

drills.notes: Opens the body and primes the kinetic chain with a heavy ball, focusing on rotational connection and a controlled arm path for recovery and foundational feel.
Promotes integrated upper and lower body rotation, encouraging better trunk flexion and arm path awareness at a moderate weight for gentle pattern work.
Helps re-establish hip rotation and trunk engagement, contributing to improved total trunk flexion and hip drive without high impact.
Targets lead leg stability and ground force absorption, aiding in the development of better peak abduction mechanics during the recovery phase.
A low-intensity drill for general recovery, promoting lower body connection and rhythm, which subtly supports overall movement efficiency.

sessions.label: Hybrid day
sessions.session_intent: Gradually increase intent and dynamic movement, balancing feel with moderate effort. Continue to target key inefficiencies and refine movement patterns.

drills.notes: Continues to prime rotational movement and arm path, but with increased reps to gradually build volume and reinforce the desired feel.
Reinforces hip drive and trunk engagement with slightly higher reps, building on the P0 foundation to enhance total trunk flexion and separation.
Introduces momentum to improve kinetic chain sequencing and encourage more dynamic trunk flexion and a connected arm path for improved horizontal abduction.
Focuses on lead leg drive and hip rotation, directly addressing peak abduction deficits and promoting efficient trunk rotation with a lighter ball for increased speed.
Emphasizes controlled yet powerful hip rotation, which is crucial for improving total trunk flexion and building a more robust hip drive for arm acceleration.
Further challenges lead leg stability and force transfer, essential for translating hip drive into effective peak abduction mechanics as intensity increases.

sessions.label: Work day
sessions.session_intent: Maximize intent expression and aggressively target movement inefficiencies. Drive high-output movements with lighter balls to achieve peak velocity and refine mechanics.

drills.notes: Serves as a high-intent primer, preparing the body for explosive rotational movements and reinforcing a connected arm path for maximum velocity output.
Aggressively works on hip-shoulder separation and trunk rotation, directly improving total trunk flexion and contributing to a more powerful kinematic sequence.
Utilizes a very light ball to maximize intent and accelerate hip rotation and lead leg block, critically addressing both peak abduction and total trunk flexion for high-speed delivery.
Builds momentum and emphasizes lead leg drive, directly contributing to improved peak abduction and effective force transfer through the kinetic chain.
A dynamic drill focused on connecting the kinetic chain for maximum intent, promoting full trunk flexion and refining the arm path from initiation to release.
A high-intent rotational drill, crucial for pushing limits in total trunk flexion and developing powerful hip drive, directly translating to increased mound velocity.
